risk_if_not_implemented: Without Contacts With Authorities, residual security, compliance, or resilience risk may accumulate, potentially leading to control failures or non-compliance.

compensating_control_1.name: Incident Stakeholder Reporting
compensating_control_1.description:
Mechanisms exist to timely-report incidents to applicable:
(1) Internal stakeholders; 
(2) Affected clients & third-parties; and
(3) Regulatory authorities.
compensating_control_1.justification: Incident Stakeholder Reporting (IRO-10) provides incident response capability that compensates for the absence of Contacts With Authorities (GOV-06) by enabling timely detection, containment, and recovery from security events in the absence of the primary control. Given the process-oriented nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.

compensating_control_2.name: Statutory, Regulatory & Contractual Compliance
compensating_control_2.description: Mechanisms exist to facilitate the identification and implementation of relevant statutory, regulatory and contractual controls.
compensating_control_2.justification: Statutory, Regulatory & Contractual Compliance (CPL-01) provides overlapping security capability that compensates for the absence of Contacts With Authorities (GOV-06) by addressing related risk objectives through an alternative control mechanism aligned with Process applicability. Given the process-oriented nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.
